title: GASTAT Health Care Statistics 2025 (Saudi Arabia)
abstract: National tables from GASTAT's Health Care Statistics 2025 publication (46 tables), covering health expense coverage, health care visit rates, dental care, self-reported unmet health/dental needs, teleconsultation and digital health access, and long-term care for older adults, disaggregated by sex, nationality, age group, and administrative region. Based on the National Health Survey 2024 and the Woman and Child Health Survey 2024.

resources.name: Health Care Statistics 2025 (XLSX, 46 tables)
resources.path: KAUST - DATA/Human Nutrition and Health/Health Care 2025 EN (1).xlsx

variables.name: coverage_basic_health_expenses_pct
variables.label: Coverage of Basic Health Expenses
variables.description: Percentage of the population (by nationality, sex, age group) covered for basic health expenses.

variables.name: internet_health_info_use_pct
variables.label: Internet Use for Health Information
variables.description: Percentage of adults who used the internet to obtain health-related information including nutrition and health promotion, by sex and age group.

access.how_to_request: Published openly by the General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT) on the Saudi Open Data platform / stats.gov.sa; no request required.

provenance.originator: General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT), Saudi Arabia
provenance.citation: GASTAT. 2025. Health Care Statistics 2025. Riyadh: General Authority for Statistics.

known_issues: Multi-sheet XLSX with 46 tables in wide, human-readable (not tidy) layout; would require reshaping before analysis.
General health-care-access statistics, not seafood/nutrition-specific; included for national health-system context per the Human Nutrition and Health domain scope.
No sub-national spatial detail beyond GASTAT's administrative regions, which do not align with Red Sea subbasin boundaries; applied uniformly across all five subbasins.
Exact source URL for this specific publication on stats.gov.sa was not confirmed during cataloging (a specific page/report ID could not be verified without internet access at cataloging time).
